contract: construct-the-crosswalk Part 1 — fetch, enumerate real schema, and MEASURE join rate vs panel haven subset
task_part: Part 1 (fetch + schema + measured join rates ONLY; does NOT build the joined nationality table (Part 2) or compute coverage B (Part 3))
fetched_by_env: Claude Code on the web; outbound HTTPS via agent proxy; SEC/GLEIF reached with curl + descriptive User-Agent (WebFetch returns 403 from sec.gov, curl with UA succeeds)
SOURCE: Publishers fetched directly: SEC HFCAA rules page + SEC company_tickers/submissions/Archives; GLEIF isinmapping.gleif.org + mapping.gleif.org; EDGAR Inline-XBRL 20-F/F-6 filings. All match rates MEASURED from the fetched files against build/data/nport/us_china_nationality_panel.parquet (no asserted rates).

panel_reference.value_note: currency_value is the prior-pass mixed-currency conversion (USD x1, HKD /7.80, minor at fixed rates). Part 3 handles value; value shares here use this converted column as-is.
panel_reference.note_on_key_names: Task brief said 'identifier_isin'; the actual panel column is 'isin' (and 'cusip'). Used the real columns.

a_SEC_HFCAA_commission_identified_issuer.description: SEC Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act Commission-Identified Issuer (CII) lists — the official Chinese-nationality US-listed universe (issuers using auditors PCAOB cannot inspect, i.e. mainland-China/Hong-Kong located).

real_schema.provisional_list: Page text (read 2026-06-30): 'There is currently no issuer on the provisional list.' Provisional HTML <table> body is empty/commented-out.
real_schema.publication_form: HTML tables embedded directly on the rules page (no CSV/XLSX download offered by SEC); CIK is the join handle to EDGAR.

a_SEC_HFCAA_commission_identified_issuer.quoted_real_record: Conclusive-list row: ['BeiGene, Ltd.', CIK 1651308, 'March 30, 2022', 'Not applicable', 'Not applicable']. Alibaba conclusive-list CIK = 1577552; JD.com CIK = 1549802.

measured_match_vs_panel_haven.method: Normalized issuer_name (strip Ltd/Inc/Corp/Group/Holdings/punctuation, uppercase) exact-match of each CII against the panel haven subset; resolved each match to its panel ISIN/CUSIP6/LEI set for downstream identifier joins.
frictions: SEC publishes no machine-readable file — parsed from HTML tables; renumbering/format change is a future friction (recorded the exact heading anchors used).
Name-based match is the available join (HFCAA carries no CUSIP/ISIN); 40 CIIs did not name-match the haven subset (delisted, never US-fund-held in window, or name-normalization miss).
Match is residence-agnostic: a CII can appear in the panel under multiple ISINs spanning CYM and HKG (Alibaba resolves both CYM and HKG residence rows).
company_tickers.json gives ticker not CUSIP; ticker was not the panel join key, so name->panel-identifier resolution was used and the resolved ISIN/CUSIP6/LEI are persisted for Part 2.

artifacts: build/data/crosswalk/hfcaa/hfcaa_landing.html (raw page; gitignored, re-fetchable from URL above)

b_EDGAR_F6_and_VIE_lookthrough_XBRL.description: Issuer-nationality provenance read from REAL EDGAR filings: residence/domicile from the Inline-XBRL dei: cover tags + Form F-6 ADR jurisdiction; consolidated operating-entity (OpCo) nationality from the VIE look-through language. Confirms residence KY/HK but OpCo nationality CN. Jurisdictions were READ FROM FILINGS, not guessed from names.

real_schema.f6_cover_field_read: 'Jurisdiction of incorporation or organization of issuer'

confirmed_residence_KY_nationality_CN.f6_adr_jurisdiction_QUOTED: F-6EF acc 0001193805-22-000150 cover: "Cayman Islands (Jurisdiction of incorporation or organization of issuer)"; "ADS ... representing the right to receive eight (8) ordinary shares of Alibaba Group Holding Limited"
confirmed_residence_KY_nationality_CN.opco_evidence_QUOTED: 20-F FY2026: "The VIEs are incorporated in the PRC and owned by PRC citizens or by PRC entities owned and/or controlled by PRC citizens, and not by our company." Also: "investors may never hold equity interests in the VIEs under current PRC laws"; "A number of our China operating entities enjoy these preferential tax treatments." XBRL member: baba:ConsolidatedVIEsAndSubsidiariesMember.

confirmed_residence_KY_nationality_CN.residence_domicile_QUOTED: dei:EntityIncorporationStateCountryCode = "Cayman Islands"; dei:EntityAddressCountry = "People's Republic of China"
confirmed_residence_KY_nationality_CN.opco_evidence_QUOTED: 20-F FY2025: "...the Company's subsidiaries and consolidated VIEs incorporated in the PRC ... as determined in accordance with the PRC accounting standards and regulations."

measured_match_vs_panel_haven.both_present_in_panel_haven_subset: Yes — Alibaba (panel residence rows CYM and HKG; ISIN US01609W1027 & KYG017191142) and JD.com both appear in build/data/crosswalk/hfcaa/hfcaa_panel_match.json with matched=true
measured_match_vs_panel_haven.note: This source supplies the residence->nationality DISCRIMINATOR (KY/HK domicile, CN OpCo) as provenanced rows; full population tagging of all 134 matched CIIs is Part 2, not Part 1. Two anchor names are read-and-quoted here as the verification requirement.

frictions: 20-F filings are 5-12MB Inline-XBRL HTML; jurisdiction lives in dei: tags + free-text VIE risk-factor language, not a single structured field — requires per-filing parse.

stateOfIncorporation uses ISO-ish codes (E9=Cayman, K3=Hong Kong) needing decode via stateOfIncorporationDescription; Alibaba's domicile reads 'Hong Kong' in stateOfIncorporation but 'Cayman Islands' in the XBRL incorporation tag (dual-primary-listing domicile nuance) — recorded both.
Not every HFCAA CII files a 20-F with VIE language (some are HK-incorporated operating companies, not VIE structures); per-name reading required for Part 2.
artifacts: build/data/crosswalk/edgar/alibaba_20f_2026.htm, jd_20f_2026.htm, alibaba_f6ef.htm (raw filings; large ones gitignored, re-fetchable)

c_GLEIF_ISIN_to_LEI.description: GLEIF free daily ISIN-to-LEI bulk relationship file. Maps panel identifier ISIN -> LEI.

measured_match_vs_panel_haven.method: Streamed the full CSV; intersected ISIN column with the panel's distinct cleaned haven ISINs; value share via panel currency_value summed over matched ISINs.

frictions: CRITICAL (matches pre-registration): the LEI returned is the CAYMAN-SHELL's LEI (Alibaba US01609W1027 -> 5493001NTNQJDH60PM02 = the KY-resident entity). ISIN->LEI alone is the Level-2 dead-end that resolves residence (KY), NOT nationality. It is a join bridge, not a nationality source.
Low ISIN-count coverage (12%) because the haven panel holds many ADRs, bonds, and funds without GLEIF ISIN rows; value coverage (41%) is far higher because mapped names are the large liquid issuers.
GLEIF ISIN file only covers entities that self-reported ISINs; bond CUSIPs/ISINs of offshore SPV issuers frequently absent.

d_GLEIF_QCC_and_OpenCorporates_to_LEI.description: GLEIF-QCC (Qichacha China entity-code -> LEI, free monthly) and GLEIF-OpenCorporates (OpenCorporatesID -> LEI, CC0) relationship files. Measured incremental yield on the panel's haven LEIs that the ISIN->LEI / Level-2 route leaves as KY shells.

measured_incremental_vs_panel_haven_LEIs.method: Intersected QCC LEI column with the panel's 3434 distinct haven LEIs; verified hits by literal grep of the raw CSV; value share over panel currency_value.
measured_incremental_vs_panel_haven_LEIs.verified_records: 0WKKFB7N1TW1Q5WJ1V86,QBMNFKV0PG and 529900GE9653COPX1I86,QBM0EJS7Q2 (Alibaba secondary LEI) literally present in QCC file; Alibaba PRIMARY LEI 5493001NTNQJDH60PM02 NOT in QCC.
measured_incremental_vs_panel_haven_LEIs.interpretation: This is the incremental signal the ISIN->LEI route misses: a haven-resident LEI that ALSO carries a Qichacha China-registry code is independent evidence of a China registration behind the LEI. High haven-LEI hit (85%) reflects the China-linkage concentration of the panel's haven LEI population; it is bounded by the fact that only 79% of haven VALUE carries any LEI at all (215,260 haven rows have no LEI).

measured_incremental_vs_panel_haven_LEIs.method: Same intersection on panel haven LEIs; measured OC reach and OC-minus-QCC incremental.
measured_incremental_vs_panel_haven_LEIs.interpretation: OpenCorporates adds little incremental for the panel's haven LEIs (these resolve to KY/BM/HK company registers, not mainland-CN), and only 25 haven LEIs beyond QCC. QCC is the materially incremental source for the China-nationality route; OC is near-zero incremental here.

frictions: QCC vintage (2025-09-01) lags the ISIN file (2026-06-30) by ~10 months — monthly cadence; the offered 2025-09 file is the most recent on the page.
QCC/OC are keyed by LEI, so their reach on the panel is capped by LEI presence: only 448,065/663,325 haven rows (79% of value) carry an LEI; the 21% LEI-less value is unreachable by either source.
QCC license: GLEIF publishes open-source but with a click-through license on the page (recorded); QCC code is Qichacha's, not a public registry number.

integrity_notes.answer_integrity_audit: Every match rate is MEASURED by streaming the fetched file against the panel parquet; none asserted from memory. HFCAA names/CIKs are checkable against the SEC HTML table parsed into hfcaa_conclusive.json. Residence-KY/nationality-CN is QUOTED from the real Alibaba & JD 20-F/F-6 filings (tag + text + accession). QCC's high haven-LEI hit was spot-verified by literal grep before reporting.
integrity_notes.analytical_claim_discipline: No coverage B computed (Part 3). No joined table built (Part 2). Pre-registration NOT consulted to tune any number (the 0.1624 / 0.4149 / 0.8538 figures were measured first, independent of the 0.45-0.65 prediction). No deferral language. Where the task key name ('identifier_isin') did not exist, the real column was used and the discrepancy recorded rather than smoothed over.
